title: Multiple Myeloma Roadmap Public Task Queue v0

tasks.task_title: Draft Multiple Myeloma Open Research Map v0.1
public_deliverables: A Markdown navigation artifact titled Multiple Myeloma Open Research Map v0.1.
A machine-readable companion record if the disease-map schema exists before drafting.
Catalog and README links that make the map findable from the myeloma program page.
Multiple Myeloma Open Research Map v0.1 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/open-research-map-v0-1.md.
Validated JSON companion at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/open-research-map-v0-1.json.
acceptance_checks: The map separates facts, derived claims, hypotheses, open questions, and do-not-use clinical material.
Every section lists source IDs or explicitly marks missing source extraction.
The map links the wedge, source registry, measurement glossary, mechanism map, public task queues, and review boundary.
No section ranks patient options, recommends therapy, recommends a trial, or claims a cure.
make validate passes.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-open-research-map-v0-1.
JSON companion validates against Disease Map Schema v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that a map section is clinically actionable.
Do not treat artifact coverage as evidence strength or biological frequency.
Do not imply the open research map is comprehensive across all myeloma settings.

tasks.task_title: Expand reusable myeloma source registry anchors
public_deliverables: New or revised source registry records for target, therapy, trial, regulatory, and dataset source classes.
A source-registry Markdown update matching the machine-readable JSON.
A short limitations note for each source class.
Source Registry v0 updated with reusable target, therapy, trial, regulatory, labeling, and dataset source anchors.
acceptance_checks: Each new source record has owner, source type, blood-cancer scope, claim uses, claim limits, URL, and access date.
Sources are public, free to reference, and do not require credentials or restricted datasets.
Claim limits explain what the source cannot prove.
make validate passes.
Completed in public artifact source-registry-v0 with reusable target, therapy, trial, regulatory, labeling, and dataset source classes.
what_not_to_infer: Do not treat a source record as evidence by itself.
Do not infer approval, efficacy, availability, eligibility, or safety from registry presence alone.
Do not add restricted or paid-access dependencies to the public workflow.

tasks.task_title: Define the first disease-map schema and companion Markdown shape
public_deliverables: A JSON schema for disease-map records or a clearly named first schema slice.
A companion README or Markdown description of required map sections.
Validator coverage for the new schema before any generator depends on it.
Disease Map Schema v0 at schemas/disease-map-schema-v0.md with a validated template record.
acceptance_checks: The schema requires disease scope, source IDs, claim level, uncertainty, limitations, and clinical-use boundary.
The schema can represent target, therapy, trial, open-question, and public-task links without patient-specific fields.
The Markdown companion explains how facts, derived claims, hypotheses, and open questions differ.
make validate passes.
Completed in public artifact disease-map-schema-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not build generation scripts before the shape is validated.
Do not include patient-specific features or candidate options in public disease-map records.
Do not flatten MRD, relapse, target status, or trial status into one generic evidence field.

tasks.task_title: Inventory the downstream schema and tooling phase before adding generators
public_deliverables: A public phase inventory that names the next target, therapy, trial, open-question, public-task, or tooling shape to define.
A decision table separating ready schema slices from blocked, deferred, and needs-human review work.
A recommendation for the next validated shape before evidence graph, trial explorer, target prioritization, extraction helper, or review packet builder work starts.
Updates to roadmap or open-research-map navigation showing that tooling waits for validated shapes.
Multiple Myeloma Schema And Tooling Phase Inventory v0 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/schema-tooling-phase-inventory-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The inventory states which schema slices already exist and which are missing.
The inventory does not add generation scripts or extract new biomedical claims.
Each next-phase candidate lists required source IDs, public safety boundary, and blocker status.
The selected next task remains research-use-only and cannot be interpreted as clinical prioritization.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-schema-tooling-phase-inventory-v0 with target-record schema selected next.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that a ready schema task is evidence strength, biological priority, patient relevance, or treatment value.
Do not build evidence graph, trial explorer, target prioritization, extraction helper, or review-packet builder tooling before the relevant shape is validated.
Do not create target, therapy, trial, or open-question records that imply actionability, availability, eligibility, efficacy, safety, or a cure claim.

tasks.task_title: Define a public target-record schema before target tooling
public_deliverables: A JSON schema for target-record records.
A Markdown companion explaining required target, source, uncertainty, limitation, and clinical-use-boundary fields.
A template target-record JSON file with public placeholder values only.
Validator coverage for records with target_record_id before target-prioritization or evidence graph tooling starts.
Catalog, README, and roadmap links that make the target-record schema discoverable.
Target Record Schema v0 at schemas/target-record-schema-v0.md with a validated template record.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The schema requires target ID, target name or symbol, disease scope, source IDs, claim level, uncertainty, limitations, review status, and clinical-use boundary.
The schema can link to claims, gaps, mechanism groups, therapy records, trial records, and public tasks without requiring those records to exist yet.
The schema does not accept patient-specific target expression, candidate options, actionability scores, treatment choices, trial matching, or real case data.
The Markdown companion states that target-record validation checks shape only, not targetability, efficacy, safety, eligibility, availability, or patient fit.
Completed in public artifact target-record-schema-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer target actionability from a valid target record.
Do not infer that a target is expressed in any person.
Do not rank targets, therapies, trials, or products.
Do not use the schema for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.

tasks.task_title: Define a public therapy-record schema after target shape
public_deliverables: A JSON schema for therapy-record records.
A Markdown companion explaining required therapy class, target-link, source, uncertainty, limitation, and clinical-use-boundary fields.
A template therapy-record JSON file with public placeholder values only.
Validator coverage for records with therapy_record_id before therapy landscape, evidence graph, or trial explorer tooling starts.
Catalog, README, and roadmap links that make the therapy-record schema discoverable.
Therapy Record Schema v0 at schemas/therapy-record-schema-v0.md with a validated template record.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The schema requires therapy ID, therapy class or modality, disease scope, source IDs, claim level, uncertainty, limitations, review status, and clinical-use boundary.
The schema can link to target records, claims, gaps, mechanism groups, trial records, and public tasks without requiring patient-specific candidate options.
The schema does not accept treatment recommendations, option rankings, dosing, eligibility, availability claims for any person, expanded-access guidance, or real case data.
The Markdown companion states that therapy-record validation checks shape only, not efficacy, safety, availability, eligibility, sequencing, or patient fit.
Completed in public artifact therapy-record-schema-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer therapy value from a valid therapy record.
Do not rank therapies, products, trials, or targets.
Do not use the schema for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.
Do not infer that any therapy is available, appropriate, safe, effective, or indicated for any person.

tasks.task_title: Define a public trial-landscape record schema after therapy shape
public_deliverables: A JSON schema for trial-landscape records.
A Markdown companion explaining registry provenance, data freshness, therapy and target links, uncertainty, limitations, and clinical-use boundary fields.
A template trial-landscape record JSON file with public placeholder values only.
Validator coverage for records with trial_landscape_record_id before trial explorer or evidence graph tooling starts.
Catalog, README, and roadmap links that make the trial-landscape record schema discoverable.
Trial-Landscape Record Schema v0 at schemas/trial-landscape-record-schema-v0.md with a validated template record.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.

The schema requires trial-landscape ID, registry source IDs, query or registry provenance, data timestamp or accessed date, disease scope, source IDs, uncertainty, limitations, review status, and clinical-use boundary.
The schema can link to target records, therapy records, claims, gaps, mechanism groups, and public tasks without requiring patient-specific eligibility or matching fields.

The schema does not accept eligibility guidance, enrollment advice, availability claims for any person, treatment recommendations, sponsor access instructions, expanded-access guidance, or real case data.
The Markdown companion states that trial-landscape validation checks shape only, not eligibility, availability, appropriateness, safety, efficacy, enrollment status, or patient fit.
Completed in public artifact trial-landscape-record-schema-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer trial eligibility from a valid trial-landscape record.
Do not infer trial availability, enrollment fit, sponsor access, safety, efficacy, or appropriateness for any person.
Do not use the schema for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.
Do not rank trials, therapies, products, or targets.

tasks.task_title: Define a public open-question record schema after trial shape
public_deliverables: A JSON schema for standalone open-question records.
A Markdown companion explaining question type, provenance, linked target, therapy, trial, claim, gap, mechanism, and task fields.
A template open-question JSON file with public placeholder values only.
Validator coverage for records with open_question_record_id before evidence graph or map-builder tooling starts.
Catalog, README, and roadmap links that make the open-question record schema discoverable.
Open-Question Record Schema v0 at schemas/open-question-record-schema-v0.md with a validated template record.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The schema requires open-question ID, question text, question type, disease scope, source IDs, uncertainty, limitations, review status, and clinical-use boundary.

The schema can link to target records, therapy records, trial-landscape records, claims, gaps, mechanism groups, and public tasks without requiring clinical priority, urgency, or patient-specific fields.
The schema does not accept clinical priority, evidence-strength ranking, urgency, treatment recommendations, trial recommendations, expanded-access guidance, or real case data.
The Markdown companion states that open-question validation checks shape only, not evidence strength, clinical importance, patient relevance, or actionability.
Completed in public artifact open-question-record-schema-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that an open question is clinically urgent because it validates.
Do not infer evidence strength, patient relevance, or actionability from an open-question record.
Do not use open-question records for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.
Do not rank questions, trials, therapies, products, or targets by clinical priority.

tasks.task_title: Create a schema-backed tooling readiness gate before first generator
public_deliverables: A public tooling readiness gate that maps evidence graph, trial explorer, target prioritization, extraction helper, and review-packet builder ideas to validated input schemas.
A no-tooling-yet decision table that names the smallest safe first tooling slice or records a blocker.
A boundary section that blocks patient-specific use, clinical recommendation, trial guidance, evidence-strength ranking, and generated claims.
Catalog, README, roadmap, and open research map links if the gate becomes a reusable public protocol artifact.
Multiple Myeloma Tooling Readiness Gate v0 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/tooling-readiness-gate-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The gate references the validated disease-map, target-record, therapy-record, trial-landscape-record, open-question-record, and public task queue shapes.
The gate distinguishes schema readiness, source coverage, review readiness, and tooling readiness without turning any into clinical actionability.
The gate either selects one smallest safe public tooling slice or records why tooling remains blocked.
The gate does not build a generator, dashboard, explorer, ranking tool, trial finder, patient matcher, or recommendation system.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-tooling-readiness-gate-v0 with review-packet builder input manifest specification selected as the next spec-only slice.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that schema readiness means tooling should generate biomedical claims.
Do not infer that graph connectivity, task priority, or review readiness is evidence strength.
Do not use a tooling gate for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.
Do not rank questions, trials, therapies, products, targets, or mechanisms by clinical priority.

tasks.task_title: Define a review-packet builder input manifest spec before builder code
public_deliverables: A public review-packet builder input manifest specification.
A field list for public artifact IDs, paths, schema IDs, source IDs, claim IDs, gap IDs, mechanism IDs, measurement IDs, task IDs, validation outputs, limitations, and review status.
Validation expectations for a future manifest before any review-packet builder code is written.
Boundary language that blocks generated claims, ranking, patient matching, clinical recommendations, trial guidance, expanded-access guidance, and real case data.
Catalog, README, roadmap, open research map, and phase inventory links if the manifest specification becomes a reusable protocol artifact.
Review-Packet Builder Input Manifest Spec v0 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/reviews/review-packet-builder-manifest-spec-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The specification references only public artifacts, public schemas, public task queues, public source IDs, and public validation outputs.
The specification preserves provenance, subtype scope, uncertainty, limitations, review status, and no-clinical-use boundaries.
The specification keeps missing inputs explicit instead of filling or generating claims.
The specification states that manifest validation is not expert review, evidence strength, clinical importance, patient relevance, actionability, or publication authorization.
No builder code, generator, dashboard, explorer, ranking tool, trial finder, patient matcher, recommendation system, or generated-claim pipeline is added.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-review-packet-builder-manifest-spec-v0 with manifest schema and validator coverage selected next.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that a manifest specification authorizes review-packet-builder code.
Do not infer evidence strength, clinical importance, patient relevance, or actionability from manifest inclusion.
Do not use a manifest for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.
Do not rank questions, trials, therapies, products, targets, sources, mechanisms, or tasks by clinical priority.

tasks.task_title: Define a validated review-packet builder manifest schema before builder code
public_deliverables: A JSON schema for review-packet builder input manifest records.
A public placeholder manifest template with no real case data and no generated claims.
Validator coverage for records with review_packet_builder_manifest_id.
A Markdown companion explaining manifest identity, packet routing, artifact inputs, schema inputs, source inputs, missing inputs, review routing, limitations, and safety boundary fields.
Catalog, README, roadmap, open research map, and phase inventory links that make the validated manifest shape discoverable.
Review-Packet Builder Manifest Schema v0 at schemas/review-packet-builder-manifest-schema-v0.md with a validated placeholder manifest record.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The schema requires manifest ID, packet type, target packet, public artifact inputs, source IDs, limitations, review status, missing-input handling, and clinical-use boundary fields.

The schema can link public artifact IDs, paths, metadata paths, schema IDs, source IDs, claim IDs, gap IDs, mechanism IDs, measurement IDs, task IDs, query-record IDs, and extraction-record IDs without requiring generated text.

The validator rejects patient identifiers, real case data, generated biomedical claims, treatment recommendations, trial recommendations, expanded-access guidance, publication authorization, rankings, clinical priority, urgency, and patient relevance fields.
No builder code, generator, dashboard, explorer, ranking tool, trial finder, patient matcher, recommendation system, or generated-claim pipeline is added.
Completed in public artifact review-packet-builder-manifest-schema-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that a validated manifest schema authorizes review-packet-builder code.
Do not infer expert review, evidence strength, clinical importance, patient relevance, actionability, or publication authorization from manifest validation.
Do not use manifest records for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.
Do not rank questions, trials, therapies, products, targets, sources, mechanisms, artifacts, tasks, or evidence by clinical priority.

tasks.task_title: Define a no-code review-packet builder dry-run plan before builder code
public_deliverables: A no-code dry-run plan for future review-packet-builder behavior using only public placeholder manifests.
A no-generated-claims policy for future builder work.
A copy, reference, and refuse table that separates source-backed public content reuse from generated biomedical text.
A missing-input handling plan that fails closed instead of filling absent artifacts, sources, limitations, review status, or validation outputs.
Roadmap, readiness-gate, task-queue, and open-research-map links that keep builder implementation blocked until the dry-run plan is reviewed.
Review-Packet Builder Dry-Run Plan v0 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/reviews/review-packet-builder-dry-run-plan-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The dry-run plan references Review-Packet Builder Manifest Schema v0 and its placeholder template as the only manifest inputs.
The plan states that future builder work may copy existing public artifact references and metadata but must not generate, rewrite, rank, summarize as advice, or strengthen biomedical claims.
The plan separates allowed copy, allowed reference, required omission, and hard refusal behavior for missing or unsafe inputs.
No builder code, generator, dashboard, explorer, ranking tool, trial finder, patient matcher, recommendation system, generated packet output, or generated-claim pipeline is added.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-review-packet-builder-dry-run-plan-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that a dry-run plan authorizes review-packet-builder code.
Do not infer expert review, evidence strength, clinical importance, patient relevance, actionability, or publication authorization from manifest validation or dry-run planning.
Do not use dry-run examples for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.
Do not rank questions, trials, therapies, products, targets, sources, mechanisms, artifacts, tasks, or evidence by clinical priority.

tasks.task_title: Run an aggregate implementation gate before review-packet builder code
public_deliverables: An aggregate implementation gate for review-packet-builder code readiness.
A decision table checking manifest schema validation, dry-run behavior, no-generated-claims policy, refusal behavior, and output boundaries.
A recommendation to either keep builder code blocked or select the smallest safe code slice.
Explicit blockers for generated claims, real case data, private records, patient-specific outputs, rankings, recommendations, and publication authorization.
Roadmap, task queue, and tooling-readiness links that preserve the no-code boundary until the gate is complete.
Review-Packet Builder Implementation Gate v0 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/reviews/review-packet-builder-implementation-gate-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The gate references Review-Packet Builder Manifest Schema v0 and Review-Packet Builder Dry-Run Plan v0.
The gate states whether builder implementation remains blocked or a smallest safe code slice is selected.
The gate does not add builder code, generated packet output, generated biomedical claims, ranking behavior, trial matching, recommendation behavior, or patient-specific interpretation.
If a future code slice is selected, it is limited to copied public identifiers, public paths, boundary text, validation status, and explicit missing-input reports.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-review-packet-builder-implementation-gate-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that an implementation gate authorizes clinical use.
Do not infer expert review, evidence strength, clinical importance, patient relevance, actionability, or publication authorization from implementation readiness.
Do not use readiness-gate outputs for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.
Do not rank questions, trials, therapies, products, targets, sources, mechanisms, artifacts, tasks, or evidence by clinical priority.

tasks.task_title: Build a deterministic review-packet manifest route-table dry-run script
public_deliverables: A deterministic local route-table dry-run script for the public placeholder manifest.
A fixture based on schemas/review-packet-builder-manifest-template-v0.json.
A refusal fixture for at least one forbidden manifest field.
Documentation stating that script output is copied-reference routing only, not a review packet, not expert review, not generated biomedical text, and not publication-ready.
Focused tests or validation checks that fail closed on missing paths, missing source IDs, missing boundary fields, or forbidden inputs.
Review-packet manifest route-table dry-run tool at tools/review_packet_manifest_route_table.py.
Placeholder route-table fixture at examples/review-packet-builder-route-table-placeholder-fixture-v0.json.
Forbidden-field refusal fixture at examples/review-packet-builder-forbidden-input-fixture-v0.json.
acceptance_checks: make validate passes.
The script reads only public repo files and requires no network, credentials, accounts, paid services, or private records.
The script output is limited to copied public identifiers, public paths, boundary text, validation status, missing-input records, and refusal reasons.
The script rejects patient identifiers, real case data, generated claims, recommendations, rankings, trial matching, expanded-access guidance, publication authorization, and private records.
No generated review packets, generated biomedical prose, generated public explainers, patient-specific outputs, ranking views, trial finder, recommendation system, or publication workflow is added.
Completed in public artifact review-packet-manifest-route-table-tool-v0 with a self-test covering forbidden fields, missing paths, unknown source IDs, and missing boundary fields.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that a route-table script is a review-packet builder.
Do not infer expert review, evidence strength, clinical importance, patient relevance, actionability, or publication authorization from route-table output.
Do not use route-table output for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, or cure claims.
Do not rank questions, trials, therapies, products, targets, sources, mechanisms, artifacts, tasks, or evidence by clinical priority.

public_deliverables: A JSON schema for route-table dry-run outputs produced by tools/review_packet_manifest_route_table.py.

Validator coverage for route-table output records before any packet assembly code can rely on them.
Documentation that route-table records are copied-reference routing only, not review packets, expert review, generated biomedical prose, recommendations, rankings, or publication-ready text.

The validator rejects generated claims, patient identifiers, recommendations, rankings, trial matching, expanded-access guidance, publication authorization, private records, and packet assembly fields.

No review packet assembly, generated prose, clinical ranking, trial finder, recommendation system, patient-specific workflow, or publication workflow is added.

what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that a route-table output schema authorizes packet assembly.
Do not infer expert review, evidence strength, clinical importance, patient relevance, actionability, or publication authorization from route-table output records.
Do not use route-table output for treatment selection, trial selection, expanded-access decisions, monitoring decisions, or cure claims.
Do not rank questions, trials, therapies, products, targets, sources, mechanisms, artifacts, tasks, or evidence by clinical priority.

A boundary table that keeps generated biomedical prose, recommendation, ranking, patient-specific output, trial matching, expanded-access guidance, and publication workflow blocked.

tasks.task_title: Draft a public-safe case-feature-bundle shape summary
public_deliverables: A public-safe summary of the private-only case-feature-bundle shape.
A do-not-upload boundary that rejects real cases, identifiers, free-text notes, dates tied to a person, and images.
Links from the case-to-cure blueprint and schema README if the shape becomes a reusable public method artifact.
Case-Feature Bundle Public Summary v0 at schemas/case-feature-bundle-public-summary-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: The artifact describes allowed field groups without soliciting case upload.
All examples are synthetic placeholders or generic field labels.
The summary states that real case data belongs only in a governed private environment.
The summary preserves measurement context, disease-state scope, source provenance, uncertainty, and limitations.
make validate passes.
Completed in public artifact case-feature-bundle-public-summary-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not publish a private case schema that can be filled with real public case data.
Do not include rare combinations, exact dates, notes, locations, provider names, or record identifiers.
Do not imply the public repo can evaluate an individual case.

tasks.task_title: Add a candidate-option scoring rubric with no-action safeguards
public_deliverables: A rubric that separates standard-care review, trial review, expanded-access review, research-only hypotheses, and no-go records.
Explicit dimensions for evidence strength, uncertainty, feasibility, measurement clarity, review owner, and overclaiming risk.
A boundary statement that scores are not recommendations and do not rank patient options.
Candidate-Option Scoring Rubric v0 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/candidate-option-scoring-rubric-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: The rubric requires candidate_type before any score is interpreted.
Expanded-access and trial-review categories require site, sponsor, clinician, and regulatory verification language.
Research-only and no-go records are first-class outcomes, not failures.
No score can be used as treatment advice, trial advice, or a patient-specific ranking.
make validate passes.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-candidate-option-scoring-rubric-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that higher score means better treatment.
Do not rank standard care, trial, expanded-access, research-only, and no-go buckets on one clinical axis.
Do not turn hypothesis scores into clinical action.
safety_boundary: Requires qualified clinician review for any real case.
Requires site or sponsor verification for any trial or expanded-access question.

tasks.task_title: Add a publication-gate checklist for case-derived public learning
public_deliverables: A publication-gate checklist for any case-derived public learning.
Fail-closed criteria for privacy, provenance, subtype scope, review status, and clinical-use boundary.
A link from the case-to-cure blueprint stage gates.
Publication-Gate Checklist v0 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/publication-gate-checklist-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: The checklist blocks PHI, raw records, re-identification keys, dates tied to a person, images, and free-text notes.
The checklist requires source IDs, subtype scope, uncertainty, limitations, and review status before publication.
The checklist distinguishes aggregate learning from patient-specific candidate options or decisions.
The checklist states that clinical, legal, regulatory, sponsor, and institutional decisions remain outside the public repo.
make validate passes.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-publication-gate-checklist-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that passing a public checklist authorizes publication of real case facts without private governance.
Do not downstream patient-specific candidate options, monitoring plans, or decisions.
Do not use the public repo as a medical record or clinical decision system.

tasks.task_title: Create a multidisciplinary multiple myeloma review packet template
public_deliverables: A reusable public review packet template for multidisciplinary myeloma review.
Sections for hematology, cellular therapy, pathology, genomics, trial operations, regulatory access, safety, ethics, and patient communication review.
A do-not-fill-publicly boundary for real case facts.
Multidisciplinary Review Packet Template v0 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/reviews/multidisciplinary-review-packet-template-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: The template is usable without private context and contains no real case data.
Each review section asks for source IDs, uncertainty, limitations, and reviewer role.
The template separates source-checked, expert-review-needed, expert-reviewed, and blocked statuses.
The template does not recommend treatment, trial enrollment, or expanded access for any person.
make validate passes.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-multidisciplinary-review-packet-template-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer expert review has occurred because a template exists.
Do not fill the public template with real case facts.
Do not treat review prompts as medical advice.

tasks.task_title: Create a case-specific private-lab blocker register
public_deliverables: A public-safe Case-Specific Private-Lab Blocker Register v0.
A stage-by-stage map from case_00 through case_14 to private-lab tasks or human-gated blockers.

Explicit blocked public outputs for real case data, patient-specific candidate options, treatment recommendations, trial recommendations, expanded-access guidance, monitoring instructions, and publication authorization.
Metadata, catalog, and README navigation for the blocker register.
Case-Specific Private-Lab Blocker Register v0 at disease-programs/multiple-myeloma/case-specific-private-lab-blocker-register-v0.md.
acceptance_checks: Every stage from case_00 through case_14 has a private-lab or human-gated blocker entry.
The register distinguishes consent, PHI, source freshness, measurement, lab validity, CDS/regulatory, feasibility, safety, and publication gates.
The register states that clinical, legal, regulatory, sponsor, institutional, treating-team, and publication decisions remain outside the public repo.
No real case data, patient identifiers, exact person-linked dates, notes, images, rare identifying combinations, private record paths, or re-identification keys are included.
No treatment recommendation, trial recommendation, expanded-access guidance, monitoring instruction, or candidate-option ranking is added.
make validate passes.
Completed in public artifact multiple-myeloma-case-specific-private-lab-blocker-register-v0.
what_not_to_infer: Do not infer that a blocker register authorizes real case processing in the public repo.
Do not infer that any private-lab task has been completed.
Do not infer clinical readiness, expert review, publication authorization, trial availability, sponsor access, patient fit, efficacy, safety, or cure from a blocker entry.

review_notes: Derived from multiple-myeloma public-roadmap-v0 and case-to-cure-pipeline-blueprint-v0.
Uses currently validated BCMA and post-CAR T gap IDs as anchors so the queue can pass the existing public task schema.
Task priority indicates public artifact readiness, not evidence strength, biological importance, treatment value, or patient relevance.
A future broader roadmap gap register is needed before this queue can be treated as comprehensive across all myeloma settings.
